questions.question: Which keyword is used to define a structure in C++?
questions.explanation: The 'struct' keyword is used to define a structure, which groups related variables of different data types under a single name.

questions.question: Which operator is used to access a member of a structure variable?
questions.explanation: The dot operator (.) is used to access members of a structure variable. The arrow operator (->) is used when accessing members through a pointer to a structure.

questions.question: When a structure variable is passed to a function by value, what happens?
choices: The function receives a pointer to the original structure
A copy of the entire structure is made for the function
The function receives a reference to the original structure
Only the first member of the structure is passed
questions.explanation: When passed by value, a complete copy of the structure is created for the function parameter. Changes to the parameter do not affect the original structure.

questions.question: How do you declare an array of 50 structure variables of type Student?
questions.explanation: An array of structures is declared the same way as any other array: the type name followed by the variable name and size in brackets — Student roster[50];

questions.question: A structure that contains another structure as one of its members is called a ___ structure.
questions.explanation: A nested structure is a structure that contains another structure as a member. You access the inner members using multiple dot operators, e.g., employee.birthDate.year.

questions.question: The ___ keyword is used to define a structure in C++.
questions.explanation: The struct keyword begins a structure definition, which groups related variables (called members) of potentially different data types into a single unit.
